​

Design and development of a real-time communication and management ecosystem for HMI Stars Consulting​

FINAL YEAR PROJECT · 2025/2026​

Hassen Abdelli & Yassine Elhadj · Defended 22 June 2026​

The Problem: Fragmented Operations​

Before this project, HMI Stars Consulting relied on email, instant messaging, and physical paperwork — resulting in critical operational failures:​

Lost Documents​

KBIS records and VAT declarations misplaced in cluttered email threads​

No Traceability​

Employee attendance logs lacked centralised tracking or audit trails​

Delayed Response​

Urgent administrative requests averaged 3–5 business days to resolve​

Challenge 1: Manual Overhead​

Operational overhead caused by paper logging and spreadsheet processing makes payroll tracking and verification slow and error-prone:​

Manual Logging​

Errors, late timesheets, and lost check-ins leading to operational delays.​

Slow Processing​

End-of-week payroll validation bottlenecks and delayed reviews.​

No History​

Lack of immediate lookup logs and digital records for compliance audits.​

Challenge 2: Administrative Data Silos​

Isolated employee logs and document tracking across separate communication platforms and spreadsheets risk data security:​

Isolated Info​

Employee profiles, attendance logs, and contracts stored separately.​

Communication Silos​

Documents and files shared via unstructured chats and email threads.​

Sync Overhead​

Manual copies required to synchronize tables across departments.​

Challenge 3: Offline Disconnections​

Remote field construction sites frequently lose cellular network coverage, causing standard client systems to fail:​

Outage Areas​

Basements, remote yards, and warehouses lose cellular signal.​

Web Crashes​

Standard web forms fail to load without active internet connection.​

Data Loss​

Timesheet entries and clock-in logs get lost on connection drops.​

Project Objectives​

Establishing the system's core operational and technical requirements:​

Unified Hub​

Centralize files and chats in one spot for immediate audit lookup.​

Offline Sync​

Implement local database buffers on mobile clients for network dropouts.​

Zero Trust Security​

Parse engine-level RLS policies to isolate tenant data.​

Methodology: 2TUP​

The Two Track Unified Process (2TUP) separates development into a functional track (requirements, use cases) and a technical track (architecture, database). Both converge during Construction.​

Inception​

2 weeks: use cases, actors, priorities.​

Elaboration​

2 weeks: sequence, class, database schema.​

Construction​

4 iterations (9 weeks): auth, attendance, chat.​

Transition​

2 weeks: testing, deploy, final report.​

Each Construction iteration targeted specific use cases, enabling incremental validation of RLS policies, state management, and offline caching before final integration.​

Functional Actors​

Defining platform roles and database access boundaries:​

Client Manager​

Registers daily check-ins, files leaves, and uploads docs using mobile app.​

HMI Manager​

Verifies company accounts, audits legal files, and manages users on web portal.​

Automation Engine​

Parses PostgreSQL security, processes file archives, and routes WS notifications.​

Ecosystem Use Cases​

Core Operations​

Authentication: Secure magic link redirect and passwordless OTP sign-in.Core Records: Employee registry construction and timesheet tracking.​

Support Sockets​

Messaging: Real-time chat with file uploads and instant document scanning.​

Mobile Application — Key Features​

Attendance & Absences​

Interactive calendar for daily pointage logging with offline caching and automatic sync on reconnection​

Real-Time Chat & Documents​

Instant messaging with file attachments, shared document panels, and an integrated camera-based document scanner​

Disciplinary Warnings​

Categorised letter templates with Gmail integration for formal dispatch to selected employee recipients​

Web Administration Platform​

[ Web Dashboard Screenshot ]​

Administrator Workspace​

The web platform provides HMI Stars staff with comprehensive oversight across all client companies:​

  • •Company Directory — registration, categorisation, and detailed workspace per client​

  • •Employee Registry — active staff management with bilingual attendance export (Excel/CSV)​

  • •Central Messaging — real-time chat with all clients and shared file archives​

  • •Global Notes & Reminders — cross-company task monitoring and urgent alert validation​

  • •Accounts Management — role control, manual email verification, and impersonation mode​

System Architecture​

Client-Server with BaaS​

Both Flutter frontends communicate with a centralised Supabase backend via REST API and WebSocket subscriptions. PostgreSQL Row Level Security enforces per-company data isolation at the database level — no company can access another's records.​

Key security guarantee: JWT tokens carry the company ID; RLS policies restrict all queries to matching records only.​

Core Technology Stack​

Framework selection for cross-platform and data reliability:​

Flutter​

Single source codebase for Android, iOS, and Web platforms.​

Supabase​

Managed cloud BaaS with WebSockets streams, Storage, and Auth.​

PostgreSQL​

Relational storage database protecting system entries.​

Password Recovery Sequence​

Secure authentication routing via URL hash parameters:​

Reset Request​

User requests recovery email link in mobile app, generating a secure token.​

Redirect Token​

Link redirects user back to app with an access token hash parameter.​

Session Verify​

Flutter captures parameters and validates session with BaaS database.​

Attendance Sync Flow​

Offline-tolerant clock-ins with automatic synchronization:​

Local Write​

Log is written to local SQLite database when offline.​

Cloud Sync​

Batch sync uploads SQLite queue once internet returns.​

Engine Validate​

Server verifies timestamp consistency before writing to ledger.​

Absence Filing Sequence​

Transactional consistency from mobile request to database logs:​

Submit Request​

Manager logs absence leave on mobile with description.​

RLS Validation​

Database RLS validates user company ID.​

Instant Broadcast​

Record is inserted and broadcast to Web Admin via sockets.​

Real-time Messaging Flow​

WebSocket broadcasts using PostgreSQL Write-Ahead Logs:​

Zero Polling​

Eliminates HTTP polling completely, saving battery and server resources.​

Push Replication​

WAL logs push updates immediately through active WebSocket connections.​

Instant Chat​

Messages render in less than 200ms in admin and mobile panels.​

Structural Design: Classic Classes​

Conceptual class models, attributes, and object-oriented methods:​

Company Model​

• companyName: String• address: String• getEmployees(): List​

Employee Model​

• name: String• contractType: Enum• getLeaves(): List​

User Model​

• role: UserRole• preferences: Map• authenticate(): boolean​

Structural Design: Logical Classes​

Mapping logical models to physical database types:​

UUID Primary Keys​

Prevents sequential guessing attacks.​

Status Enums​

Ensures type check compliance at database level.​

Tenant Anchors​

company_id foreign key references on all tables.​

Database Design (Physical)​

PostgreSQL tables constructed in the cloud instance:​

companies Table​

• id: uuid PRIMARY KEY• name: text• address: text​

employees Table​

• id: uuid PRIMARY KEY• company_id: uuid FK• full_name: text​

attendance Table​

• id: uuid PRIMARY KEY• employee_id: uuid FK• check_in: timestamptz​

Database Security​

Isolating tenant records via Row-Level Security policies:​

Row-Level Security​

RLS blocks direct client queries from reading another tenant's files.​

Tenant Isolation​

Enforced via auth.jwt() claims to verify company ID matches query.​

Granular Access​

Restricts read/write access based on active role credentials.​

Technical Elaborations: DB Views​

Computed joins and virtual tables mapping relational structures:​

Employee Analytics​

Aggregates worked hours, overtime logs, and leave status counts.​

Real-time Chat Feeds​

Computed join combining user messages with active company IDs.​

Audit Compliance Log​

Virtual table tracking validation actions of legal document files.​

System Deployment​

[ Cloud Infrastructure Setup ]​

Deployment Pipelines​

Distribution pipelines for mobile and web clients:​

  • •Web Panel — Hosted on Vercel utilizing global Edge networks for sub-second page loads.​

  • •Database Vault — Hosted on Supabase cloud servers, using WAL logs replication.​

  • •Mobile App — Compiled into native binaries (APK/IPA) using Flutter's native compiler.​

  • •Global Notes & Reminders​

  • •​

System Demonstration​

[ ▶ Play System Demonstration Video ]​

Ecosystem Walkthrough​

Watch real-time synchronization between mobile and web clients:​

  • •1. Offline Pointage — Field staff clock in offline; timesheets cache locally in SQLite database.​

  • •2. Background Sync — Automatically uploads offline queue to PostgreSQL once connection restores.​

  • •3. Real-time Sockets — Chat messages and file uploads broadcast instantly via WebSockets.​

  • •Global Notes & Reminders​

  • •​

Testing & Validation Results​

18/18 Test Cases Passed​

All functional tests passed across both platforms, covering authentication, employee CRUD, attendance logging, real-time messaging, document scanning, RLS isolation, and offline caching.​

Usability Evaluation​

Ten participants rated all five criteria — Learnability, Responsiveness, Feedback, Navigation Consistency, and Real-time Reliability — as Good.​

Conclusion & Future Work​

The HMI Stars ecosystem successfully centralises communication, document traceability, and employee management — replacing fragmented manual processes with a secure, real-time platform.​

Short Term​

Automated email digests and daily status reports via Supabase Edge Functions​

Medium Term​

Automated PDF payslip generation from approved monthly attendance logs​

Long Term​

AI-powered document classification and OCR to streamline document intake​

FLUTTER​

SUPABASE​

POSTGRESQL​

RLS​

REAL-TIME​

​

HMI Stars: Web & Mobile Application​

Thank you for your attention.​

DEVELOPED BY HASSEN ABDELLI​

Hassen Abdelli & Yassine Elhadjand recommendations.​
